mainEntity.name: How much does a BMW Z8 4.9 engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned S62B50 engine typically costs £6,000–£10,000 supply only, with rebuilt units reaching £14,000. Fitting adds £1,500–£2,500. Use our calculator for a figure specific to your Z8.

mainEntity.name: Is the BMW Z8 4.9 (S62) engine reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: For a high‑performance engine from this era, the S62 has earned a reputation for reliability — if maintained correctly. The main watchpoints — VANOS, rod bearings and timing chain guides — are well‑understood and manageable with preventative maintenance. Buy on full service history and budget for the big three items.

mainEntity.name: What are the common problems with the BMW Z8 4.9 engine?

acceptedAnswer.text: VANOS system wear (the most common issue), rod bearing wear, timing chain guide degradation, oil leaks, and cooling system deterioration. The engine is fundamentally robust but demands meticulous care.

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing a Z8 4.9 engine, or should I scrap the car?

acceptedAnswer.text: Z8 values range from £80,000 to over £200,000+ — a £7,000–£10,000 recon is a small fraction of the car's value, making replacement a clear decision for all but the most challenging examples. This is well within our 70% BMW scrap threshold.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my BMW Z8 4.9 engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: Listed on the VIN plate and vehicle documentation. The Z8 4.9 engine code is S62B50. Tell us your registration date and we'll confirm it for free.

mainEntity.name: Can I prevent the common failures on a Z8 4.9?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes — the single most important prevention is regular oil changes with the correct 10W‑60 oil and shortened intervals. Have the VANOS rebuilt with upgraded seals, replace the rod bearings preventatively, and inspect the timing chain guides at 100,000 miles. At the first sign of rattle, knock or loss of power, book an inspection immediately.

replacementCosts.figuresNote: Figures above reflect the current S62B50 generation. A reconditioned S62 engine is a significant investment due to the engine's rarity and the specialist knowledge required.
replacementCosts.labourEstimate: Fitting a Z8 4.9 engine typically takes 14–18 hours at £65–£95/hr (independent) or £110–£160/hr (London/SE dealer). M‑specific engine work requires specialist knowledge.

problems.title: VANOS System Wear
problems.symptoms: Loss of low‑end power, hesitation on acceleration, rough idle, metallic rattle from the front of the engine, engine warning light.
problems.repairCost: £800–£1,800 (VANOS rebuild with upgraded seals and anti‑rattle kit)
problems.replacementCost: N/A — repair only (if caught before catastrophic failure)

urgency.label: Check
urgency.text: book inspection urgently if symptoms appear

problems.recommendation: The S62's VANOS system is the most common failure point. Earlier S62s had oil consumption issues and VANOS cold‑start rattle. Specialist companies offer rebuilt units with upgraded seals and anti‑rattle kits. At the first sign of loss of power or rattle, have the VANOS inspected — catching it early prevents more serious engine damage.

problems.title: Rod Bearing Wear
problems.symptoms: Knocking or rumbling noise from the engine, particularly when cold; metallic debris in the oil; sudden engine seizure.
problems.typicalMileage: 60,000–100,000 miles (can occur at any mileage if neglected)

urgency.label: Immediate
urgency.text: if you hear knocking, stop driving and book an inspection today

problems.recommendation: The S62 engine is known to have issues with rod bearing wear. While not as common as VANOS failure, bearings are guaranteed to be worn by now in most normal, properly maintained S62s. Regular oil changes with the correct 10W‑60 oil are crucial. Many specialists recommend replacing the rod bearings preventatively — a relatively small cost compared to a full engine replacement. If you hear knocking, do not drive the car.

problems.title: Timing Chain Guide Degradation
problems.symptoms: Rattling noise on cold start, metallic ticking, engine warning light, loss of power.
problems.replacementCost: N/A — repair only (if caught before chain jumps)

urgency.label: Immediate
urgency.text: if you hear rattle, book an inspection today

problems.recommendation: The plastic timing chain guides degrade over time, causing noise and potential catastrophic failure if neglected. Preventive replacement around 100,000 miles is strongly recommended. At the first sign of rattling on cold start, have the guides inspected immediately.

buyingChecks.title: Buying This Z8 4.9? Check These First
buyIf: * VANOS rebuilt with upgraded seals and anti‑rattle kit
* Timing chain guides inspected or replaced
* Full BMW service history with correct 10W‑60 oil
avoidIf: * Loss of power or rattle — VANOS may be failing — walk away
* Knocking from the engine — rod bearings may be failing — walk away
* Cold‑start rattle — timing chain guides may be failing
* Poor service history — S62 engines demand meticulous care

repairBuyOrReplace.closingVerdict: The Z8 is BMW's halo roadster — an aluminium‑bodied, V8‑powered masterpiece designed by Henrik Fisker and powered by the S62 from the E39 M5. Only 5,703 were built between 2000 and 2003. The S62 is a robust engine — if maintained correctly. The main watchpoints — VANOS, rod bearings and timing chain guides — are all well‑understood and manageable with preventative maintenance. Buy on full service history and budget for the big three items. A well‑maintained Z8 is a rewarding, appreciating modern classic.

cta.label: → Compare Z8 4.9 engine prices before you buy

buyingChecklist.intro: Before you buy a used BMW Z8 4.9 (or before you commit to an engine replacement), check these:
items: * Cold Start — Listen for rattling; VANOS or timing chain indicator
* Knocking — Listen for knocking or rumbling; rod bearing indicator
* Power Delivery — Check for hesitation or loss of low‑end power; VANOS indicator
* Service History — including engine era (S62B50) and confirmed maintenance with 10W‑60 oil
* VANOS History — Rebuilt with upgraded seals?
* Timing Chain Guide History — Inspected or replaced?
* Engine Warning Light — Have codes been read? Any unresolved VANOS or timing issues?
